decisions.title: Contracts are the only normative source of truth
decisions.summary: All normative rules and structures must live in contracts/.

decisions.title: docs/ is generated team interface and archive only
decisions.summary: Human-facing docs are generated views or archival artifacts, never normative.

decisions.title: Drift is blocked by hard local guards
decisions.summary: generate --check, decision-guard, and hard-cutover-guard are mandatory local gates.

decisions.title: Mapper-Toolkit v2 ships without FishIT-Player backward-compatibility guarantees
decisions.summary: Runtime tooling is now centered on Mapper-Toolkit contracts and portable workflows; legacy FishIT-Player specific functions and path conventions are not a compatibility target.

decisions.title: Mapper-Toolkit UX baseline is portable Linux CLI plus arrow-key terminal UI
decisions.summary: Toolkit delivery must include a bootstrap installer and a user-friendly terminal interface that can be navigated with arrow keys while orchestrating underlying scripts.

decisions.title: Mapper-Toolkit default UX is a Textual main.py TUI with contract-backed command matrix
decisions.summary: The default tui entrypoint is scripts/device/toolkit/main.py; users execute commands through screen actions and parameter dialogs without shell input, while a per-screen command matrix is rendered from a central contract catalog.

decisions.title: Mapper-Toolkit prioritizes a live triage console for mapping-engineer runtime diagnosis
decisions.summary: Triage becomes a first-class workflow with presets, anomaly rules, and incident bundles to accelerate UI↔backend correlation debugging and mapping output generation.

decisions.title: Mapper-Toolkit replay stage uses named baselines and warn-then-block regression policy
decisions.summary: Replay and validation are first-class workflows: seeds from triage/incident-pack are replayed and diffed against named versioned baselines, with local advisory reporting and CI blocking on critical assertion failures.

decisions.title: Capture completeness, deterministic request/response correlation, and host-app second-activity embedding are mandatory
decisions.summary: Runtime capture must emit request_id/response_id-linked chains with pipeline-ready quality gates and generic host coverage while exposing a stable second-activity integration contract for future FishIT-Player embedding.

decisions.title: Deterministic probe pipeline is canonical: strict scope, phase markers, provenance graph, and dedup quality gates
decisions.summary: Mapper-Toolkit evolves to deterministic probe execution with strict target host scoping, canonical request deduplication, first-class probe phases, provenance graph outputs, replay-driven header minimization, and pipeline-ready thresholds for duplicate ratio and phase completeness.

decisions.title: Jetpack WebKit and Cronet are integrated as isolated mapper modules without replacing EinkBro core
decisions.summary: The app keeps EinkBro as browser foundation and layers two additive modules: mapper-webkit-compat for runtime WebView compatibility/reset hooks and mapper-native-network-cronet for mapper-owned replay/validation requests behind a feature-flagged client abstraction.

decisions.title: Runtime capture/export cutover uses phase-scoped normalized raw-event SSOT with candidate-only body storage and atomic finalization
decisions.summary: Provider-draft-ready exports require deterministic phase resolution, target-host-aware classification, canonical request/response linkage, explicit truncation semantics, provenance registry, replay-minimized requirements, and latest artifact publication only after successful finalization.

decisions.title: Regression cutover hardens runtime export with canonical host taxonomy, phase-window resolution, and explicit extraction/truncation contracts
decisions.summary: Runtime exports now use canonical target_* host classes, independent phase resolution from explicit marker windows, explicit truncation/original length semantics, candidate-only extraction attempt events, and fixture-based regression checks for ZDF-scoped provider-draft runs.
